codeReview.summary: Implementation fully satisfies all requirements from the feature description and validation contract. The worker added CursorContext and ClaudeContext variants to ExportFormat enum, updated FromStr parsing with case-insensitive matching, implemented generate_cursor_context() and generate_claude_context() with proper markdown structure including metadata headers, blocking issues sections (correctly filtering unresolved meh/no ratings), hotspots sorted by count descending, and element-specific notes grouped by selector. The punk/zine tone is present throughout ('vibes are off', 'Where the action is', 'Deep cuts', 'raw truth'). The error message correctly lists all 5 formats. The blocking criteria matches the MCP definition (!is_resolved() && (meh || no)), and the hotspots algorithm is identical to MCP's implementation (element-type only, counted by selector, sorted desc). 32 comprehensive TDD-style tests cover all scenarios: format parsing, blocking logic, hotspot computation, empty states, positive-only data, mixed ratings, element grouping, and distinct format styling.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed AGENTS.md conventions: no unwrap() in production code, used ? operator for error propagation, punk/zine energy in user-facing strings, and TDD approach with tests first. All patterns were already documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: export.rs lines 28-32 (error handling with ?), lines 115-117 (punk tone), tests at bottom of file (TDD pattern)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The chrono crate was already a dependency (version 0.4 with serde feature), so no new dependencies were needed for timestamp generation. This was correctly identified by the worker.

Worker correctly reused existing patterns: is_resolved() method from spike.rs, load_spikes() from storage.rs, and the same hotspot algorithm as MCP get_hotspots (count element spikes by selector, sort desc).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: export.rs lines 318-330 (compute_hotspots using HashMap counter pattern identical to mcp.rs)

summary: Feature fully implemented and validated. 32 new tests pass, all 187 tests total pass (155 existing + 32 new). Build succeeds with zero errors. All VAL-EXPORT assertions (001-006) and cross-area assertions (VAL-CROSS-001, VAL-CROSS-003) are satisfied. The two context formats are distinct in tone and structure while sharing the same data model and blocking/hotspot logic as the MCP server.
